response: {"purpose":"Figure 2e presents Irreproducible Discovery Rate (IDR) analysis applied to eCLIP fold-enrichment values to assess the reproducibility of binding site identification across biological replicates. For RBFOX2, IDR is evaluated both between two biological replicates and between a true replicate and an IgG-only negative control, providing a direct contrast between genuine signal and background noise. The same IDR framework is applied to SLBP biological replicates, demonstrating that SMInput normalization yields reproducible enrichment signals for proteins with distinct binding profiles. This analysis matters because it provides a rigorous, quantitative measure of experimental reproducibility, validating that SMInput-normalized eCLIP consistently identifies the same high-confidence binding sites across independent experiments while distinguishing them from non-specific IgG background.","title":"IDR Analysis Confirms Reproducibility of eCLIP Biological Replicates"}
